title: Implement .beam parser and chunk decoding
purpose: The loader is the front door — nothing runs until we can read a .beam file off disk and turn it into structured data. This brief delivers the container parser (FOR1/BEAM header + chunk extraction) and all chunk decoders (atoms, instructions, strings, imports, exports, lambdas, literals, line info, operands). It does NOT resolve imports or register modules — that is B-004. The split keeps the parser pure-input and independently testable against real .beam files from gleam build.
task: Implement the .beam chunked binary parser in parser.rs and the chunk/instruction decoders in decode.rs. The parser reads the FOR1/BEAM header, iterates chunk headers, and extracts each chunk as a typed struct with raw byte payloads. The decoder processes each chunk type: registers atoms in the AtomTable, decodes the Code chunk into Instruction values using compact term encoding, decompresses and deserializes the LitT chunk, and parses ImpT/ExpT/FunT/StrT/Line chunks into structured data. Define the Instruction enum and all operand types. The loader module's public API is a load_beam_chunks() function that takes bytes and an AtomTable reference and returns a ParsedModule (all chunks decoded, imports unresolved).

requirements.title: FOR1/BEAM container parser
requirements.spec: WHEN given a byte slice THE SYSTEM SHALL parse the .beam chunked binary format: verify the FOR1 header, verify the BEAM form type, and extract each chunk as a (tag, data) pair. IF the header is missing or the form type is not BEAM THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return LoadError::InvalidFormat. It SHALL NOT attempt to interpret chunk contents — only extract them by tag.
acceptance: parse_beam_chunks(valid_beam_bytes) returns a Vec of (FourCC, &[u8]) chunk pairs
parse_beam_chunks(bytes_with_bad_magic) returns Err(LoadError::InvalidFormat)
parse_beam_chunks(bytes_with_wrong_form_type) returns Err(LoadError::InvalidFormat)
Chunk tags are preserved as 4-byte ASCII identifiers (e.g. b"AtU8", b"Code", b"StrT")

requirements.title: Atom chunk decoder
requirements.spec: WHEN the parser extracts an Atom or AtU8 chunk THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the atom table entries and register each atom in the provided AtomTable. The decoder SHALL return a Vec<Atom> preserving the file's atom ordering (index 0 is the module name). It SHALL NOT skip or silently drop malformed atom entries — each produces a LoadError::DecodeError.
acceptance: decode_atom_chunk(chunk_bytes, &atom_table) returns Vec<Atom> with length matching the atom count header
Each decoded atom is interned in the AtomTable and resolvable back to its string
The first atom in the returned vec is the module name atom
Truncated or malformed atom entries produce LoadError::DecodeError

requirements.title: ImpT, ExpT, FunT chunk decoders
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the ImpT chunk into ImportEntry structs (module atom, function atom, arity), the ExpT chunk into ExportEntry structs (function atom, arity, label), and the FunT chunk into LambdaEntry structs (function atom, arity, label, num_free). All atom references SHALL be resolved against the file's local atom table (decoded in R2). IF an atom index is out of range THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return LoadError::DecodeError.
acceptance: decode_import_chunk returns Vec<ImportEntry> with module: Atom, function: Atom, arity: u8
decode_export_chunk returns Vec<ExportEntry> with function: Atom, arity: u8, label: u32
decode_lambda_chunk returns Vec<LambdaEntry> with function: Atom, arity: u8, label: u32, num_free: u32
Out-of-range atom indices produce LoadError::DecodeError

requirements.title: StrT and Line chunk decoders
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the StrT chunk as a raw byte buffer (string table) and the Line chunk into source location entries (file name indices and line numbers). Both SHALL be stored in the ParsedModule for downstream use. IF the StrT or Line chunks are absent THE SYSTEM SHALL use empty defaults — their absence is not an error.
acceptance: decode_string_chunk returns a Vec<u8> byte buffer
decode_line_chunk returns a Vec<LineInfo> with file reference and line number
Missing StrT chunk results in an empty byte buffer, not an error
Missing Line chunk results in an empty Vec<LineInfo>, not an error

requirements.title: LitT chunk decoder (compressed literals)
requirements.spec: WHEN the LitT chunk is present THE SYSTEM SHALL decompress its payload using zlib and deserialize the external term format entries into a Vec<Literal>. Each literal is a self-contained term value (integer, atom, tuple, binary, list, map) used as instruction operands. IF decompression fails or the external term format is malformed THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return LoadError::DecodeError.
acceptance: decode_literal_chunk decompresses a zlib-compressed payload and returns Vec<Literal>
Integer, atom, binary, tuple, and list literals round-trip correctly
Corrupted zlib data produces LoadError::DecodeError
Malformed external term format produces LoadError::DecodeError

requirements.title: Instruction enum and compact term encoding decoder
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an Instruction enum covering the BEAM opcodes that Gleam emits (label, func_info, move, call, call_only, call_ext, call_ext_only, call_last, call_ext_last, return, allocate, allocate_zero, deallocate, test_heap, put_list, put_tuple2, get_tuple_element, get_hd, get_tl, type tests, comparisons, select_val, select_tuple_arity, jump, gc_bif, bif, send, loop_rec, wait, try/catch, binary ops, map ops, make_fun2, call_fun, apply, badmatch, case_end, if_end, raise). Each instruction's operands SHALL be decoded using the BEAM compact term encoding (tag byte, variable-length integers, extended tags for literals/atoms/labels/registers). Unknown opcodes SHALL produce LoadError::DecodeError rather than being silently skipped.
acceptance: Instruction is a public enum in beamr::loader::decode
decode_instructions(code_bytes, &atoms, &literals) returns Vec<Instruction>
Compact term encoding correctly decodes: small integers (tag 0), atoms (tag 1), X registers (tag 2), Y registers (tag 3), labels (tag 4), extended literals (tag 7+0), extended list (tag 7+1)
A known .beam file from gleam build decodes without errors
Unknown or unsupported opcodes produce LoadError::DecodeError with the opcode number

requirements.title: Code chunk to instruction stream
requirements.spec: WHEN the Code chunk is decoded THE SYSTEM SHALL produce a Vec<Instruction> indexed by label number for jump/call resolution. The instruction stream SHALL preserve label positions so that label N maps to a code offset. IF the Code chunk header (sub_size, instruction_set, opcode_max, label_count, function_count) contains unsupported values THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return LoadError::DecodeError.
acceptance: Code chunk header fields are parsed: instruction_set, opcode_max, label_count, function_count
Labels in the instruction stream are addressable by their label number
instruction_set value of 0 is accepted (standard BEAM)
An unsupported instruction_set value produces LoadError::DecodeError

requirements.title: ParsedModule struct and public load API
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a ParsedModule struct that aggregates all decoded chunks: module name (Atom), atoms (Vec<Atom>), instructions (Vec<Instruction>), imports (Vec<ImportEntry>), exports (Vec<ExportEntry>), lambdas (Vec<LambdaEntry>), literals (Vec<Literal>), string table (Vec<u8>), and line info (Vec<LineInfo>). The public API SHALL be a load_beam_chunks(bytes: &[u8], atom_table: &AtomTable) -> Result<ParsedModule, LoadError> function. It SHALL NOT resolve imports or store the module in a registry — that is B-004.
acceptance: ParsedModule is a public struct with all fields listed above
load_beam_chunks(valid_beam_bytes, &table) returns Ok(ParsedModule)
load_beam_chunks(garbage_bytes, &table) returns Err(LoadError::InvalidFormat)
ParsedModule.name is the first atom from the atom chunk
ParsedModule.imports contains unresolved ImportEntry values (no resolution attempted)

requirements.title: Snapshot tests against real .beam files
requirements.spec: WHEN testing the loader THE SYSTEM SHALL include at least one test that loads a .beam file produced by gleam build + erlc and verifies the parsed output. Test .beam files SHALL be committed to a tests/fixtures/ directory. Tests SHALL verify: atom count, export names, import count, and instruction count for a known module.
acceptance: A tests/fixtures/ directory exists under crates/beamr/ with at least one .beam file
A test loads the fixture .beam file via load_beam_chunks and asserts atom count matches expected
The test asserts at least one expected export name is present
The test asserts the import count matches expected
cargo test -p beamr loader passes with all tests green

boundaries: SHALL NOT resolve imports against loaded modules or the BIF registry (B-004 scope)
SHALL NOT store the parsed module in the module registry (B-004 scope)
SHALL NOT validate instruction operands beyond basic decoding (B-004 scope)
SHALL NOT implement Term values — literals are stored in a loader-local Literal type, not as runtime Terms (B-005/B-006 scope)
SHALL NOT implement any BIFs or native functions (B-008 and later scope)
verification: cargo test -p beamr loader passes with all tests green
A .beam file from gleam build + erlc loads successfully via load_beam_chunks
Code inspection: no import resolution logic in parser.rs or decode.rs
Code inspection: loader/mod.rs contains only pub mod, pub use, and the load_beam_chunks function signature
